data_in.description: targetTable.csv — feature table containing the six features to annotate
xcmsSet (xset) object — peak-picked LC-MS data processed with xcms
RAMClustR (RC) object — pseudo-MS/MS spectra derived from RAMClustR processing
LipidPos fragment library — ion fragment database for lipid annotation

data_out.description: Annotation results table listing feature IDs, candidate lipid identities, and matching scores, with three features annotated and LPC(14:0) as rank-1 for feature 3
Ranked candidate annotations for each of the six features

domain_knowledge: MetaboAnnotatoR performs all-ion fragmentation (AIF) metabolite annotation by matching observed fragment ions against curated ion fragment databases such as LipidPos, ranking candidates by cosine similarity or related matching scores.

The annotateRC function requires three synchronized data objects: an xcms peak table (peak m/z, retention time, and intensity), a RAMClustR object containing pseudo-MS/MS spectra (clustered fragment ions per feature), and a specified fragment library.
LipidPos is a lipid-specific fragment ion database; using it biases results toward lipid hits and may exclude non-lipid metabolites even if they match better in other libraries.
Rank-1 annotation status means the candidate ranked highest by the matching algorithm; this does not guarantee correctness without manual verification of matched ion patterns.
The reported benchmark result (three of six features annotated, LPC(14:0) rank-1 for feature 3) serves as the reproducibility target and should be matched exactly by the annotateRC run.

direct_checks: verify file targetTable.csv exists in the repository
verify file_format_is targetTable.csv a valid CSV with at least 6 rows (features)
verify that xcmsSet (xset) object file exists in the repository or is loadable from the package
verify that RAMClustR (RC) object file exists in the repository or is loadable from the package
verify that LipidPos library files are present or accessible within the MetaboAnnotatoR package
script_runs: annotateRC function executes without error when called on targetTable.csv, xset, RC, and LipidPos libraries
output_matches_reference: annotateRC produces exactly 3 annotated features (no more, no fewer)
output_matches_reference: feature 3 is assigned LPC(14:0) as the rank-1 (top-ranked) annotation
value_in_range: annotation confidence/score for the three features meets expected thresholds (requires parameter-sensitive specification of score cutoff)
expert_review: Assess whether the three annotated features and their ranked assignments are chemically and metabolomically plausible given the input xcms peak-picking and RAMClustR pseudo-MS/MS spectra
Verify that LPC(14:0) assignment to feature 3 is consistent with expected fragment ion patterns and mass accuracy for a lipid of that composition
Confirm that the four unannotated features represent genuine metabolites or artifacts that appropriately fall below the annotation threshold

cmd: library(MetaboAnnotatoR); library(xcms); library(RAMClustR); targetTable <- read.csv('{inputs.target_table}'); load('{inputs.xcms_object}'); load('{inputs.rc_object}'); lipidLib <- readRDS('{inputs.lipid_library}'); results <- annotateRC(targetTable, xset, RC, lipidLib); print(results); write.csv(results, 'annotation_results_{task_id}.csv', row.names=FALSE)

expected_outputs: Annotation results table listing feature IDs, candidate lipid identities, and matching scores, with three features annotated and LPC(14:0) as rank-1 for feature 3

finding: When annotateRC is applied to six lipidomics features using the LipidPos library, three features receive lipid annotations, with LPC(14:0) assigned as the rank 1 annotation for feature 3 (468.3095 m/z, 82.92009 s).
inputs: targetTable.csv — feature table containing the six features to annotate
xcmsSet (xset) object — peak-picked LC-MS data processed with xcms
RAMClustR (RC) object — pseudo-MS/MS spectra derived from RAMClustR processing
LipidPos fragment library — ion fragment database for lipid annotation
methodology_summary: Load the targetTable.csv feature list and the pre-computed xcmsSet and RAMClustR objects.
Specify the LipidPos fragment ion library as the annotation database.
Execute the annotateRC function to compute fragment-ion matching scores and rank candidate lipid identities.
Extract ranked annotation results for all six features.
Validation: Verify that exactly three features receive lipid annotations and that feature 3's top-ranked candidate is LPC(14:0).
missing_information: No documentation of version history, changes, or updates to MetaboAnnotatoR

task_description: Run the annotateRC function on six features from targetTable.csv against xcmsSet and RAMClustR objects using LipidPos fragment libraries, and reproduce the reported result of three annotated features with LPC(14:0) ranked first for feature 3.

task_inputs.label: Feature table with six features to annotate

task_objective: Execute annotateRC on the bundled targetTable.csv, xset, and RC objects using LipidPos libraries and verify that exactly three of six features are annotated with LPC(14:0) as rank-1 for feature 3.

task_outputs.label: Annotation results with ranked candidates and lipid identities

title: Reproduce the lipid annotation results for six ESI+ features using annotateRC with LipidPos libraries

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Annotation results table listing feature IDs, candidate lipid identities, and matching scores, with three features annotated and LPC(14:0) as rank-1 for feature 3, Ranked candidate annotations for each of the six features.
workflow_description: 1. Load the targetTable.csv feature table and the pre-processed xcmsSet (xset) and RAMClustR (RC) objects into R. 2. Configure the LipidPos fragment libraries as the annotation database source. 3. Execute the annotateRC function to match fragment ions from the six features against the LipidPos library. 4. Retrieve and rank candidate annotations by matching score for each feature. 5. Verify that three of six features received lipid annotations and confirm LPC(14:0) appears as the rank-1 candidate for feature 3.
